items.title: Intelligence prompt formatting — split slice formatters behind existing facade
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Intelligence/Slice Complexity
items.notes: Scope: `server/intelligence/formatters.ts` and future `server/intelligence/formatters/<slice>.ts` files. Current complexity: one large formatter owns dispatch, cold-start behavior, token budget, and every slice renderer. Behavior-preserving path: keep `formatForPrompt()` as the only public facade and move two or three low-risk section renderers into slice-owned formatter modules first. Introduce a typed formatter registry only after snapshots prove output parity. Tests/guardrails: golden prompt snapshots for compact/standard/detailed verbosity, token-budget tests, cold-start tests, and no prompt text changes unless explicitly approved. || DONE 2026-06-09: extracted the low-risk `contentPipeline`, `siteHealth`, and `operational` prompt section renderers into adjacent formatter modules (`server/intelligence/formatter-content-pipeline.ts`, `formatter-site-health.ts`, `formatter-operational.ts`) while keeping `formatForPrompt()` in `server/intelligence/formatters.ts` as the sole public facade that still owns section ordering, cold-start handling, section filtering, and token-budget truncation. Moved shared percentage rendering to `server/intelligence/formatter-shared.ts` so extracted renderers preserve exact text output without duplicating logic. Added exact-output parity coverage for compact/standard/detailed representative slices in `tests/unit/intelligence-formatters.test.ts`, and updated the pr-check 'assembled-but-never-rendered slice fields' rule plus its tests to include extracted formatter modules so future slice-field drift is still caught after the split. Verification: `npx vitest run tests/unit/intelligence-formatters.test.ts tests/unit/formatters-prompt-format.test.ts tests/token-budget.test.ts tests/intelligence-integration.test.ts`, `npx vitest run tests/pr-check.test.ts tests/unit/intelligence-formatters.test.ts tests/unit/formatters-prompt-format.test.ts tests/token-budget.test.ts tests/intelligence-integration.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, and a full `npx vitest run` attempt that again reproduced unrelated existing integration `EADDRINUSE` failures across fixed-port suites outside this formatter blast radius.

items.title: Intelligence facade — introduce typed slice metadata registry incrementally
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Intelligence/Slice Complexity
items.notes: Scope: `server/workspace-intelligence.ts`, `shared/types/intelligence.ts`, `server/intelligence/formatters.ts`, slice assembler imports. Current complexity: slice names, prompt-formattable status, required options, assembly dispatch, and formatting order are spread across arrays and switches. Behavior-preserving path: add a server-side registry with assembler, prompt formatter metadata, `promptFormattable`, `requires.pagePath/siteId`, and timeout. Keep shared consts as the public type source initially; start with assembly dispatch only, then formatter dispatch later. Tests/guardrails: contract test that every `INTELLIGENCE_SLICES` value has a registry entry; TypeScript should fail on missing assembler metadata; no call-site behavior changes in first PR. || DONE 2026-06-09: introduced `server/intelligence/slice-metadata-registry.ts` as the typed server-side authority for slice assembly dispatch and required option metadata, then migrated `buildWorkspaceIntelligence()` to consume that registry instead of the large per-slice switch. Kept `shared/types/intelligence.ts` as the public slice constant/type source for this phase and limited the extraction to assembly concerns only. Preserved `siteHealth`'s timeout behavior by moving it into the registry via a shared helper that degrades to skip-on-timeout, logs exactly once, and swallows late rejections after timeout so the refactor does not double-log or surface unhandled behavior. Added focused coverage in `tests/unit/workspace-intelligence.test.ts` for registry/`INTELLIGENCE_SLICES` lockstep, required-options metadata for `pageProfile`/`pageElements`/`siteInventory`, and the `siteHealth` timeout plus late-rejection regression path. Verification: independent adversarial review found no remaining issues after the timeout/logging fix; `npx vitest run tests/unit/workspace-intelligence.test.ts tests/unit/workspace-intelligence-extended.test.ts tests/intel-prompt-equivalence.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, and a full `npx vitest run` attempt that again reproduced unrelated existing integration `EADDRINUSE` failures across fixed-port suites outside this intelligence blast radius.

items.title: Server domain shape — extract approval batch lifecycle services from thick route code
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/approvals.ts`, approval stores, page-state sync, activity, broadcasts, and Webflow apply behavior. Current complexity: approval create/respond/apply previously spread page status derivation, activity, team email, Webflow writes, outcome tracking, recommendation resolution, and deliverable mirror sync across route code and multiple domain modules. Behavior-preserving path: extract `approval-batch-mutations.ts` and `approval-batch-apply.ts`; routes become thin adapters calling `createApprovalBatchForClient`, `respondToApprovalItem`, and `applyApprovedBatchItems`. Split into two PRs: create/respond first, apply/Webflow side effects second. Tests/guardrails: approval create/respond/apply integration tests, partial apply retry tests, unified inbox respond parity, page-state aggregation contract, and broadcast assertions. || PARTIAL 2026-06-10: completed the lowest-risk admin create/delete extraction only. Added `server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-admin-mutations.ts` to own send/delete lifecycle behavior for admin approval batches: approval-batch creation/deletion, deliverable mirror sync, page-state tracking/cleanup, client notification, activity logging, and `WS_EVENTS.APPROVAL_UPDATE` broadcasts. Rewired only the admin create/delete routes in `server/routes/approvals.ts` to use the shared helper; left public respond behavior on the existing `approval-batch-respond` path and left apply/Webflow side effects untouched. Added focused parity coverage in `tests/unit/approval-batch-admin-mutations.test.ts` and kept route-level mutation safety/approval route tests green. || PARTIAL 2026-06-10 (phase 2): extracted the public per-item approval response lifecycle into `server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-item-respond.ts`. The helper now owns client approval/rejection/revert item writes, aggregated page-state derivation for multi-field pages, rejection-note refreshes, client activity logging, team approval/changes-requested notifications, and `WS_EVENTS.APPROVAL_UPDATE` broadcasts. Rewired only `PATCH /api/public/approvals/:workspaceId/:batchId/:itemId` to use the helper; left whole-batch respond on `approval-batch-respond` and left `/apply` Webflow/outcome/recommendation side effects untouched. Added focused unit coverage in `tests/unit/approval-batch-item-respond.test.ts` and kept public approval broadcast/activity route tests green. || PARTIAL 2026-06-10 (phase 3): extracted the public `/api/public/approvals/:workspaceId/:batchId/apply` lifecycle into `server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-apply.ts`. The helper now owns client apply precondition guards, static-page and real-CMS Webflow writes, partial-success result shaping, applied-item page-state/live tracking, SEO change records, activity logging, deliverable mirror flip-on-full-success semantics, outcome tracking, recommendation resolution, and `WS_EVENTS.APPROVAL_APPLIED` / `WS_EVENTS.PAGE_STATE_UPDATED` broadcasts. Rewired only the public apply route in `server/routes/approvals.ts` to use the helper and added focused unit coverage in `tests/unit/approval-batch-apply.test.ts` while keeping route-level apply guard/broadcast/lifecycle/recommendation parity suites green. || DONE 2026-06-10: closed the remaining respond-lifecycle split by introducing `server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-response-lifecycle.ts` as the single route-facing/respond-propagation facade for approval-batch decision handling. Repointed `server/routes/approvals.ts` and the approval-batch deliverable adapter shared helper to the facade so whole-batch decisions and per-item decisions now hang off one inbox-domain lifecycle authority, while keeping `approval-batch-respond.ts` and `approval-batch-item-respond.ts` as implementation-detail helpers beneath that facade. Preserved response shapes, page-state sync semantics, activity logging, team notification ownership, and `WS_EVENTS.APPROVAL_UPDATE` broadcast behavior. Updated the route-to-service extraction contract to the consolidated authority and only flipped this roadmap item to `done` once the remaining intended respond-lifecycle closure was actually in place.

items.title: Server domain shape — unify schema plan lifecycle mutations
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/webflow-schema.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/schema-plan-dual-write.ts`, schema plan store/job interactions, broadcasts, and deliverables. Current complexity: plan role updates, send-to-client, activation, delete, conflict checks, feedback handling, activity, intelligence invalidation, and deliverable sync were previously split across route handlers plus separate admin/respond helpers. Behavior-preserving path: add `server/domains/schema/schema-plan-lifecycle.ts` with `updatePlanRoles`, `sendSchemaPlanToClient`, `activateSchemaPlan`, `deleteSchemaPlanForSite`, and `respondToSchemaPlan`. Keep existing routes and response shapes. Tests/guardrails: schema plan send/feedback/delete integration tests, active-job conflict contract, deliverable sync event assertions, and state transition validation. || PARTIAL 2026-06-10: extracted the admin schema-plan mutation quartet into `server/domains/schema/schema-plan-admin-mutations.ts`. The helper now owns plan role updates, send-to-client status flips, activation, delete/retract of the entire plan, generation-job conflict checks, deliverable sync/mirroring/cancellation, schema-plan broadcasts, snapshot-deleted broadcast, client notification, activity logging, and intelligence invalidation/deduping around preview-batch cleanup. Rewired only the admin `PUT /api/webflow/schema-plan/:siteId`, `POST /send-to-client`, `POST /activate`, and `DELETE /api/webflow/schema-plan/:siteId` routes to use the helper; left public feedback separate while the remaining lifecycle seam was evaluated. Added focused unit coverage in `tests/unit/schema-plan-admin-mutations.test.ts` and kept schema mutation safety plus public schema-plan route parity suites green. || DONE 2026-06-10: completed the intended lifecycle unification by introducing `server/domains/schema/schema-plan-lifecycle.ts` as the route-facing schema-domain facade and `server/domains/schema/schema-plan-feedback.ts` as the shared feedback/conflict helper used by internal consumers that must avoid the adapter/send-to-client cycle. Repointed `server/routes/webflow-schema.ts` to the lifecycle facade, moved `server/routes/deliverables.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/send-to-client.ts`, and the schema-plan deliverable adapter onto the schema-domain feedback helper, and removed the old inbox-local `schema-plan-respond.ts` split authority. Preserved existing route URLs, response/error shapes, deliverable sync behavior, broadcasts, activity logging, and background-job conflict semantics; intentionally left schema generation plus page-level publish/retract/rollback flows untouched because they are separate schema operations rather than plan-lifecycle mutations. Added focused coverage in `tests/unit/schema-plan-lifecycle.test.ts` for feedback/broadcast/conflict behavior and updated route-to-service extraction contracts to the new authority.

items.title: Server jobs — move legacy inline `/api/jobs` bodies into runner modules
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/jobs.ts`, newer runner pattern in `server/routes/webflow-seo-jobs.ts`, and job modules for seo-audit, image compression, bulk alt text, bulk SEO fixes, and sales reports. Current complexity: `/api/jobs` mixes validation, job creation, dispatch, long-running execution, result shaping, and cancellation semantics; newer jobs already use dedicated runner modules. Behavior-preserving path: introduce a small job-handler registry and move legacy bodies into `*-job.ts` modules by job family. Keep `/api/jobs` as auth/validation/dispatch adapter. Split into 3-5 small PRs; do not migrate all job types at once. Tests/guardrails: route start tests per job type, active-job conflict tests, cancellation semantics where cancellable, result shape snapshots, `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES` lifecycle contract coverage. || PARTIAL 2026-06-09: extracted only the `sales-report` job family into `server/sales-report-background-job.ts` and rewired the `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.SALES_REPORT` branch in `server/routes/jobs.ts` to validate inputs then dispatch through the helper. Preserved the global job scope, start response shape (`{ jobId }`), terminal job messages, result payload shape, persisted report-file format (including file-only `createdAt`), and error behavior. Added focused parity coverage in `tests/unit/sales-report-background-job.test.ts` for success/error job transitions plus persisted-file/result-shape separation, and kept the existing `/api/jobs` sales-report mutation-safety integration coverage in place. Verification for this partial phase: `npx vitest run tests/unit/sales-report-background-job.test.ts tests/integration/legacy-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, an independent adversarial review with no remaining Critical/Important findings, and a clean full `npx vitest run` pass during final commit verification. || PARTIAL 2026-06-09 (phase 2): extracted the single-asset `compress` job family into `server/webflow-image-compress-background-job.ts` and rewired the `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.COMPRESS` branch in `server/routes/jobs.ts` to keep only request validation plus `{ jobId }` start response dispatch. Preserved the exact legacy job contract: 3% skip threshold behavior, skip/success/error terminal messages, success and skip result payload shapes, upload/delete ordering, temp-file handling, and `images_optimized` activity logging. Added focused runner parity coverage in `tests/unit/webflow-image-compress-background-job.test.ts` for success, already-optimized skip, and download-failure error paths, while keeping the existing route-level mutation-safety coverage in `tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts` and `tests/integration/legacy-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts` green. Verification for this partial phase: `npx vitest run tests/unit/webflow-image-compress-background-job.test.ts tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts tests/integration/legacy-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, independent adversarial review with no Critical/Important findings, and a full `npx vitest run` attempt that again reproduced unrelated existing fixed-port integration `EADDRINUSE` failures outside this jobs blast radius (including `tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts` on port `13210` plus other occupied integration ports). || PARTIAL 2026-06-09 (phase 3): extracted the `bulk-compress` job family into `server/webflow-bulk-compress-background-job.ts` and rewired the `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.BULK_COMPRESS` branch in `server/routes/jobs.ts` to keep only request validation, duplicate-job guard, and `{ jobId }` start response dispatch. Preserved the current internal `localhost` `/api/webflow/:workspaceId/compress/:assetId` call path, per-asset result ordering, numeric-only `totalSaved` accumulation, progress message format, terminal done/error messages, summary `images_optimized` activity, and partial-failure behavior where one asset error does not fail the whole job. Added focused runner parity coverage in `tests/unit/webflow-bulk-compress-background-job.test.ts` for result ordering, summary activity, and per-asset error continuation, while keeping `tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts` green. Verification for this partial phase: `npx vitest run tests/unit/webflow-bulk-compress-background-job.test.ts tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, independent adversarial review with no Critical/Important findings, and a full `npx vitest run` attempt that again reproduced unrelated existing fixed-port integration `EADDRINUSE` failures outside this jobs blast radius (including `tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts` on port `13210` plus many other occupied integration ports). || PARTIAL 2026-06-09 (phase 4): extracted the `bulk-alt` job family into `server/webflow-bulk-alt-background-job.ts` and rewired the `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.BULK_ALT` branch in `server/routes/jobs.ts` to keep only request validation, duplicate-job guard, and `{ jobId }` start response dispatch. Preserved the one-time workspace/intelligence context assembly, temp-file path/cleanup behavior, ordered per-asset result array, `Generated i/N alt texts` progress message, terminal `Done — X/N updated` message, summary `images_optimized` activity, and soft-failure handling for download/generation errors. Added focused runner parity coverage in `tests/unit/webflow-bulk-alt-background-job.test.ts` for one-time context assembly, ordered results, summary activity, and per-asset soft failures, while keeping `tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts` and `tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts` as route-level contract coverage. Verification for this partial phase: `npx vitest run tests/unit/webflow-bulk-alt-background-job.test.ts tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, independent adversarial review with no Critical/Important findings, and a full `npx vitest run` pass or explicit documentation of any unrelated pre-existing suite-health noise if it recurs. || PARTIAL 2026-06-09 (phase 5): extracted the `seo-audit` job family into `server/seo-audit-background-job.ts` and rewired the `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.SEO_AUDIT` branch in `server/routes/jobs.ts` to keep only request validation, duplicate-job guard, token precondition, and `{ jobId }` start response dispatch. Preserved the exact legacy audit flow: initial `Scanning pages...` running message, snapshot save before post-audit side effects, suppression-aware `effectiveResult`/`previousScore` handling, `audit_completed` activity, `handleOnDemandSeoAuditResult`, `WS_EVENTS.AUDIT_COMPLETE` broadcast, best-effort recommendation regeneration, best-effort client notifications, terminal result shape `{ ...effectiveResult, previousScore, snapshotId }`, and `Audit failed` error contract. Added focused parity coverage in `tests/unit/seo-audit-background-job.test.ts` for snapshot-before-side-effect ordering and terminal result/error behavior, while keeping `tests/contract/seo-audit-background-job.test.ts` and `tests/integration/legacy-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts` as route-level contract coverage. Verification for this partial phase: `npx vitest run tests/unit/seo-audit-background-job.test.ts tests/contract/seo-audit-background-job.test.ts tests/integration/legacy-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, independent adversarial review with no Critical/Important findings, and a full `npx vitest run` pass or explicit documentation of any unrelated pre-existing suite-health noise if it recurs. || DONE 2026-06-09 (phase 6): extracted the remaining `bulk-seo-fix` family into `server/webflow-bulk-seo-fix-background-job.ts`, introduced `server/legacy-jobs-runner-registry.ts` as the small start-handler registry for all extracted legacy `/api/jobs` families, and reduced `server/routes/jobs.ts` to auth/validation/dispatch responsibilities for those legacy types. Preserved the existing bulk SEO contract: published-path-aware page resolution, one-time seoContext assembly before the page loop, knowledge/persona-aware prompt composition, per-page soft failures, page-state + `seo_updated` writes on success, `WS_EVENTS.PAGE_STATE_UPDATED` broadcast, intelligence invalidation, recommendation resolution by applied page IDs, and unchanged `{ jobId }` start / terminal result shapes across the legacy families. Added focused parity coverage in `tests/unit/webflow-bulk-seo-fix-background-job.test.ts`, updated the source-contract guards in `tests/batch2-caller-contracts.test.ts` and `tests/contract/page-intelligence-seo-editor-correctness.test.ts`, and updated the intelligence-consumer inventory/docs for the new low-level runner. Verification for the completing phase: `npx vitest run tests/unit/webflow-bulk-seo-fix-background-job.test.ts tests/batch2-caller-contracts.test.ts tests/contract/page-intelligence-seo-editor-correctness.test.ts tests/contract/intelligence-consumer-inventory.test.ts tests/integration/legacy-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts tests/integration/bulk-seo-fix-resolves-recommendations.test.ts tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, independent adversarial review with no remaining Critical/Important findings, and a full `npx vitest run` pass during final commit verification.

items.title: Server Webflow assets — extract shared image optimization service
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Server Domain Shape
items.notes: Scope: `server/routes/webflow-alt-text.ts`, `server/routes/jobs.ts`, Webflow asset/CMS modules, `repairCmsReferences`, image compression/upload/delete flows. Current complexity: SVG/JPEG/PNG/WebP branches, temp-file handling, upload/delete behavior, and CMS reference repair are duplicated between route and job code; bulk-compress currently calls an internal HTTP route instead of the domain function directly. Behavior-preserving path: extract `server/domains/webflow-assets/image-optimization.ts` with `compressImageBuffer`, `replaceCompressedAsset`, and `repairCmsReferences`; route and jobs call the service directly. Tests/guardrails: unit tests for extension handling and skip thresholds; integration-style mocks for upload failure, CMS repair failure, and old asset preservation. || PARTIAL 2026-06-09: completed the first safe extraction phase only. Added `server/domains/webflow-assets/image-optimization.ts` with shared `compressImageBuffer()` and `repairCmsReferences()` helpers, then migrated only `server/routes/webflow-alt-text.ts` to consume them. Preserved compress route payloads, skip thresholds, SVG failure-as-skip behavior, upload/delete ordering, CMS repair semantics, old-asset preservation behavior, the no-`fileName` fallback filename contract, and error shapes. Added focused unit coverage in `tests/unit/webflow-image-optimization-service.test.ts` for jpg/png/svg extension handling, URL-source fallback naming, skip thresholds, and SVGO failure parity, while keeping existing `webflow-alt-text` integration suites green. Verification for this partial phase: `npx vitest run tests/unit/webflow-image-optimization-service.test.ts tests/integration/webflow-alt-text-routes.test.ts tests/integration/webflow-alt-text-routes-extended.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, and a clean full `npx vitest run` pass during final commit verification. || PARTIAL 2026-06-09 (phase 2): migrated the single-asset `/api/jobs` `compress` path in `server/routes/jobs.ts` onto the shared `compressImageBuffer()` helper without changing the job's legacy behavior. Preserved the job-specific 3% raster/SVG skip threshold, the legacy skip label (`Already optimized` for raster and SVG), SVG failure-as-error semantics via throw-through behavior, start response shape (`{ jobId }`), terminal success/skip/error payloads, upload/delete ordering, temp-file handling, and `images_optimized` activity logging. Added targeted parity coverage in `tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts` for the exact job-level already-optimized skip contract, plus helper-level override coverage in `tests/unit/webflow-image-optimization-service.test.ts` for legacy threshold, SVG skip-label, and throw-on-SVGO-failure behavior. Verification for this partial phase: `npx vitest run tests/unit/webflow-image-optimization-service.test.ts tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts`, `npx vitest run tests/integration/legacy-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, independent adversarial review with no Critical/Important findings, and a full `npx vitest run` attempt that again reproduced unrelated existing fixed-port integration `EADDRINUSE` failures outside this refactor's blast radius (including `tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts` on port `13210` plus other unrelated occupied ports). || DONE 2026-06-09: completed the remaining scope by extracting shared `replaceCompressedAsset()` authority into `server/domains/webflow-assets/image-optimization.ts`, migrating `/api/webflow/:workspaceId/compress/:assetId`, `startWebflowImageCompressJob()`, and `startWebflowBulkCompressJob()` onto the shared replacement flow, and removing the bulk-compress internal HTTP round-trip from the legacy jobs path. Preserved route response shapes, single-job terminal payloads/messages, bulk result ordering and summary activity, CMS reference repair behavior, old-asset preservation rules, temp-file cleanup authority, and existing compression thresholds per caller. Added focused parity coverage for shared replacement semantics in `tests/unit/webflow-image-optimization-service.test.ts`, single-job replacement failure handling in `tests/unit/webflow-image-compress-background-job.test.ts`, direct bulk-compress service execution in `tests/unit/webflow-bulk-compress-background-job.test.ts`, and updated `tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts` to exercise the direct bulk-compress execution path. Verification for completion: `npx vitest run tests/unit/webflow-image-optimization-service.test.ts tests/unit/webflow-image-compress-background-job.test.ts tests/unit/webflow-bulk-compress-background-job.test.ts`, `npx vitest run tests/integration/media-jobs-mutation-safety.test.ts tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts tests/integration/webflow-alt-text-routes.test.ts tests/integration/webflow-alt-text-routes-extended.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, `npx vite build`, `npm run pr-check`, `npm run verify:feature-flags`, `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`, independent adversarial review pending before PR publication.

items.title: Test guardrails — add shared source-contract helper for string-based wiring tests
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Test/Guardrail Leverage
items.notes: Scope: `tests/bridge-wiring.test.ts`, `tests/bridges-simple.test.ts`, `tests/cache-invalidation-cluster.test.ts`, and other source-sniffing tests with `readFile-ok` comments. Current complexity: many tests repeat `readFileSync`, bounded section extraction, and `toContain/not.toContain` assertions, spreading platform intent across noisy implementation details. Behavior-preserving path: add `tests/helpers/source-contracts.ts` with `readProjectFile`, `boundedSection`, `expectContainsAll`, and `expectOmitsAll`; migrate bridge/cache tests first. Tests/guardrails: run the migrated tests plus `npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts`; keep helper additive so existing source-contract tests can migrate gradually. || DONE 2026-06-09: added `tests/helpers/source-contracts.ts` with `readProjectFile`, `boundedSection`, `expectContainsAll`, and `expectOmitsAll`; migrated the bridge/cache source-contract cluster (`tests/bridge-wiring.test.ts`, `tests/bridges-simple.test.ts`, `tests/cache-invalidation-cluster.test.ts`) without changing assertions. Remaining source-sniffing tests can migrate opportunistically when adjacent guardrails are touched.

items.title: Test guardrails — extract one WS/event contract parser for coverage tests and pr-check

Current complexity: duplicate parsers collect `WS_EVENTS`, broadcasts, frontend handlers, and exemption lists; this overlaps directly with the frontend invalidation registry opportunity. Behavior-preserving path: extract `tests/helpers/ws-contracts.ts` with `parseWsEvents`, `collectBroadcasts`, and `collectHandlers`; let both tests consume the helper. Later extend it with an event-to-query-key expectation table once the frontend registry exists. Tests/guardrails: run broadcast-handler pairs, ws invalidation coverage, and pr-check tests; ensure CSS class/string false positives remain excluded. || DONE 2026-06-09: extracted shared WS contract parser logic into `scripts/ws-contract-parser.ts` so both static tests and `scripts/pr-check.ts` use the same `WS_EVENTS`, centralized handler, broadcast, and frontend subscription parsing. Migrated `broadcast-handler-pairs`, `ws-invalidation-coverage`, and pr-check's WS event loader/centralization rule without changing exemptions or event semantics. Targeted verification: `npx vitest run tests/integration/broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts tests/contract/ws-invalidation-coverage.test.ts tests/pr-check.test.ts`.

items.title: Test infrastructure — simplify integration server port ownership
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Test/Guardrail Leverage
items.notes: Scope: `tests/integration/helpers.ts`, `tests/meta-port-uniqueness.test.ts`, `scripts/pr-check.ts`, and integration tests using `createTestContext(PORT)`. Current complexity: many tests allocate literal ports, requiring meta-test and pr-check enforcement plus `port-ok` comments. Behavior-preserving path: introduce `createEphemeralTestContext()` or `tests/helpers/ports.ts` allocator while keeping literal support during migration. First PR should only add the helper and migrate one representative integration file; broad migration can be opportunistic. Tests/guardrails: `tests/meta-port-uniqueness.test.ts`, a representative integration test, and smoke tests; ensure parallel Vitest behavior remains stable. || DONE 2026-06-09: added the lock-backed `tests/helpers/ports.ts` allocator and `createEphemeralTestContext(import.meta.url, ...)` wrapper in `tests/integration/helpers.ts`, with explicit release on `ctx.stopServer()` plus one-context-per-file runtime protection. Updated `tests/meta-port-uniqueness.test.ts` to treat `createEphemeralTestContext(import.meta.url)` as a first-class valid ownership strategy while rejecting invalid or duplicate ephemeral calls, and clarified the existing `Port collision in integration tests` rule copy in `scripts/pr-check.ts`. Migrated `tests/integration/fixture-public-pricing-route.test.ts` as the representative first adopter and added allocator coverage in `tests/unit/test-ports.test.ts`. Targeted verification: `npx vitest run tests/unit/test-ports.test.ts tests/meta-port-uniqueness.test.ts tests/integration/fixture-public-pricing-route.test.ts tests/pr-check.test.ts`; `npm run typecheck`; `npx vite build`; `npm run pr-check`; `npm run verify:feature-flags`; `npm run verify:coverage-ratchet`.

items.title: Test guardrails — table-drive background job lifecycle coverage

items.notes: Scope: `tests/contract/background-job-coverage-contract.test.ts`, `shared/types/background-jobs.ts`, and integration tests around job mutation safety/lifecycle. Current complexity: manual `COVERAGE_SIGNALS` and repeated assertions for job type/status/progress/message/result behavior. Behavior-preserving path: add a test-only registry mapping each `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.*` entry to lifecycle expectations and signal files; use table-driven assertions. Tests/guardrails: background job coverage contract, lifecycle integration tests, and feature flag/job type verification; keep `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES` as the product authority. || DONE 2026-06-09: added `tests/helpers/background-job-test-matrix.ts` as the shared test-side lifecycle registry keyed by `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES`, with signal-file ownership plus pinned label, cancellable, and result-behavior expectations per job type. Rewired `tests/contract/background-job-coverage-contract.test.ts` to consume the matrix instead of a hand-maintained local map, and table-drove the lifecycle integration checks for cancellation metadata, label parity, and result-behavior categories in `tests/integration/background-jobs-lifecycle.test.ts`. Targeted verification: `npx vitest run tests/contract/background-job-coverage-contract.test.ts tests/integration/background-jobs-lifecycle.test.ts tests/unit/background-jobs.test.ts`.

items.title: Test guardrails — add pr-check fixture DSL and generated docs drift test
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Test/Guardrail Leverage
items.notes: Scope: `tests/pr-check.test.ts`, `scripts/pr-check.ts`, `scripts/generate-rules-doc.ts`, `docs/rules/automated-rules.md`. Current complexity: pr-check tests are large and noisy, and docs drift is verified manually through `npm run rules:generate`. Behavior-preserving path: add an `expectRuleCases(rule, cases)` fixture DSL for compact positive/negative cases; add a contract test that renders generated rules content and compares to committed `automated-rules.md` without writing. Tests/guardrails: `npx vitest run tests/pr-check.test.ts`, `npm run rules:generate`, and `git diff --exit-code docs/rules/automated-rules.md`. || DONE 2026-06-09: exported `renderAutomatedRulesDoc()` from `scripts/generate-rules-doc.ts` while preserving CLI generation, added a no-write docs drift test against committed `docs/rules/automated-rules.md`, and introduced `expectRuleCases()` in `tests/pr-check.test.ts` with the Global keydown customCheck block migrated as the first representative fixture table. Targeted verification: `npx vitest run tests/pr-check.test.ts`; `npm run rules:generate && git diff --exit-code docs/rules/automated-rules.md`.

items.title: C3 (audit item #12) — extract publishPostToWebflow domain service; auto-publish → background job
items.notes: DONE 2026-06-10. Extracted ONE shared publish service `server/domains/content/publish-post-to-webflow.ts` (`publishPostToWebflow()`) consumed by BOTH publish paths, mirroring `send-post-to-client.ts`. Fixed three drifts: (1) silent fire-and-forget auto-publish in `server/routes/content-posts.ts` (was ~:369-441, `createCollectionItem(...).then()` at ~:386, hatch at ~:385) now runs as a background `CONTENT_PUBLISH` job (`server/content-publish-job.ts`, new BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES entry: label 'Publishing to Webflow', non-cancellable, domain-store) following the C2-migrated runner pattern (createJob + setImmediate dispatched from the route, idempotent guard on `!webflowItemId`); failures now surface as job `error` + activity, never silent. (2) field-map drift — auto-publish wrote a strict subset (missing `summary` from brief + `featuredImage`); the unified service builds the ONE manual-superset field map, covered by a new field-map parity contract test `tests/integration/publish-service-field-parity.test.ts`. (3) skipped follow-on — `queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns` now lives inside the shared service so it fires on BOTH paths (contract test in `outcome-publish-triggers-rec-regen.test.ts` expanded). Sync-vs-job decision: manual publish stays SYNCHRONOUS (single foreground Webflow round-trip, UI awaits inline `{ success, post }`); only auto-publish became a job. Post status `approved` stays TERMINAL — publish tracked via webflowItemId/publishedAt/publishedSlug, no state-machine change. `recordAction` getActionByWorkspaceAndSource dedup guard preserved for job-retry safety. `CONTENT_PUBLISHED` broadcast + `content_published` activity + `invalidateContentPipelineIntelligence` moved into the service (single site). Migrated/updated legacy suites: content-posts-workflow (auto-publish now asserts job lifecycle + FM-2), outcome-publish-triggers-rec-regen, canonical-page-address-contract, workspace-intelligence-freshness-wiring, background-job-test-matrix; journey-content-publish / content-publish-writes / content-publish-action-tracking / content-posts-lifecycle pass unchanged (manual route keeps its response contract). Gates: typecheck + vite build + pr-check (0 errors) + full contract suite (562) + content/publish/jobs integration shard (696) green.

items.title: C4 (audit item #16) — persist AI review verdicts + scraped SERP/reference source text
items.notes: DONE 2026-06-11. (1) AI review verdicts: POST /api/content-posts/:wsId/:postId/ai-review now persists the post-provenance-marking verdict pack as `StoredAIReview` on new `content_posts.ai_review` column (migration 132) — provenance keys only ever stored pass=false + humanReviewRequired (content-quality-grounding preserved at storage layer). Route also writes `post_ai_review` activity (admin-only, NOT client-visible) and broadcasts via existing notifyContentUpdated/CONTENT_UPDATED (frontend half already wired in useWsInvalidation registry). Response contract `{ review, evidence }` unchanged. (2) Scraped source text: brief generation job persists C1's BriefScrapeEnrichment output as `BriefSourceEvidence` on new `content_briefs.source_evidence` column — scraped reference bodyText, real SERP snippets + fetchedAt (previously dropped at the generateBrief boundary), style-example pages; both standalone + request paths via one `persistBriefSourceEvidence` seam before the existing brief_generated broadcast. regenerateBrief carries sourceEvidence forward (metadata-preservation rule). Zod schemas (storedAiReviewSchema, briefSourceEvidenceSchema) cross-referenced against shared/types/content.ts with optional fields per schema-vs-stored-shape rule; parseJsonSafe with context everywhere. Public boundary: public-content.ts strips sourceEvidence from client brief GET and aiReview from client post GET + client-edit response (routes pass full objects through — would otherwise ship ~25KB scraped competitor text + admin QA verdicts to clients). Enables real-text evidence ledger #27 (parked — not built). Tests: tests/integration/c4-persist-review-results.test.ts (12 tests: persistence survives fresh GET, provenance posture, FM-2 no-persist on schema failure, both brief paths, scraper-degradation NULL column, stored-shape Zod roundtrips, public-boundary strips); updated stale pre-C4 assertion in content-posts-ai-fix.test.ts that encoded the discard-on-close behavior. Gates: typecheck + vite build + pr-check (0 errors) + content/public-content integration shard (42 files / 641 tests) green.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Completed items from 🎯 Keyword Value — propagation, money, GSC signals, client display
sprints.rationale: Completed items extracted from active sprint sprint-keyword-value-amplification-2026-06-05 during roadmap cleanup so active roadmap views show remaining work only.
sprints.notes: Extracted on 2026-06-11. Original sprint retained pending/in-progress/deferred items only.

items.title: Value-score breakdown — return + label the components instead of a bare 0-100
items.notes: Wave 1 (foundation). computeKeywordValueScore already computes commercialValue (intent × CPC), demand, winnability, and the local multiplier internally, then discards them. Return + label them so the Hub/brief/client show WHY a low-volume keyword outranks a high-volume one ('commercial intent, $9 CPC, winnable KD 24, local 1.5×'). Unblocks the client display (kwv-client-why-worth-it) and richer briefs. No new data. || PLACEMENT [exists-pattern]: OpportunityScore.components (buildComponents) already computes+renders this client-side ('Why this is #1' OverviewTab, StrategyKeywordDrawer 'See the numbers'). Work = expose the SAME components on the Hub valueScore (drawer), not a new breakdown. Depends on the score-consolidation decision (kwv-one-score-everywhere). || DONE 2026-06-05 (PR 2, branch feat/kwv-value-breakdown): keywordValueReasons helper (server/scoring/keyword-value-score.ts) computed server-side, serialized as valueReasons onto the KCC row (finalizeDraftRow) + the client strategy explanation (keyword-strategy-ux.ts), rendered in both drawers (StrategyKeywordDrawer 'See the numbers' + KeywordDetailDrawer 'Why this score', blue=data). Flag-gated on keyword-value-scoring.

items.title: Feed real CPC into the content-gap value score (fix the cpc:undefined hard-pass)
items.notes: Wave 1. keyword-strategy-enrichment hard-passes cpc:undefined into computeKeywordValueScore, so every content-gap value score uses the CPC_UNKNOWN=0.5 mid-band proxy instead of real money. ContentGap + the matched pageMap entry already carry cpc. Pass it through so commercial gaps reflect commercial value. Correctness fix that makes the score honest before propagating it. || PLACEMENT [new, ~1-line]: populate OpportunityInput.cpc for the content_gap branch (cpc plumbing already exists in opportunity-value.ts/roi.ts). No surface. || DONE 2026-06-05 (PR 1, branch feat/keyword-value-legible-dollarized): content_gaps.cpc column + ContentGap/StrategyContentGap types + content-gaps.ts mapper + enrichment populate (domain-hit + API paths) + pass cpc into the value scorer, all in one lockstep commit. cpc is score-only (intentionally NOT raw-exposed on the public content-gap projection).

items.title: Dollar value per keyword + portfolio 'revenue at stake' rollup
items.notes: Wave 2 (money; biggest client wow). Per-keyword '#7 → page-1 ≈ +$340/mo' = CTR-curve uplift (ctr-curve.ts) × monthly volume × valuePerClick (cpc × INTENT_WEIGHT, opportunity-value.ts). Plus a portfolio headline 'close your top N gaps ≈ $X/mo (~$Y/yr ad-equivalent)' reusing roi.ts adSpendEquivalent. Pure reuse of the existing EMV math + CTR curve — no GA4/provider call. Surfaces on Hub, briefs, client briefing, AI context. || PLACEMENT [mostly exists]: ROIDashboard/computeROI is the ONE client $ engine (organicTrafficValue, adSpendEquivalent, per-page trafficValue/mo). DO NOT build a second dollar engine. NEW = only a per-KEYWORD $/mo line (StrategyKeywordDrawer + a Hub $ column) reusing roi.ts avgCPC + the OV CTR-uplift. Watch the contradictory-$ trap next to milestone-attribution.ts. || DONE 2026-06-05 (PR 3, branch feat/kwv-dollar-value): ONE keywordDollarValue helper (server/scoring/keyword-value-money.ts) — currentMonthly = clicks×cpc (equivalence test: == roi.ts trafficValue), upsideMonthly = impressions × ctrAt-uplift × cpc (realized $, NO intentWeight). cpc joined from page_keywords onto KCC metrics.cpc + strategy rows. Per-keyword 'Revenue potential' $ on both drawers (emerald). Portfolio revenueAtStake (Σ upsideMonthly) as a 4th ROIDashboard StatCard. No second $ engine, no new endpoint (rides /api/public/roi + the strategy serialization).

items.title: Client: plain-language 'why this keyword is worth it' badges
items.notes: Wave 4 (client display). Turn the opaque 0-100 into a human reason: 'High-value: people searching this are ready to buy (commercial intent), it's local to your area, and it's winnable — competitors aren't strong here.' Reuses kwv-value-breakdown + ScoringContext posture/city/state already computed per request. Build alongside the client keyword display. || PLACEMENT [exists]: StrategyKeywordDrawer 'Why it's in the strategy' + buildOvGainString phrases + 'Why this is #1' bars already render plain-language reasons. Fold reason badges into those existing rows (and OutcomeWinEntry/ranking-mover narratives), not a new card. || ROADMAP AUDIT 2026-06-11: confirmed complete. Client-facing keyword value reasons are computed, rendered in the strategy keyword drawer, and covered by component tests.

items.notes: client_deliverable + _item tables (111/112); shared/types/client-deliverable.ts; server/client-deliverables.ts store; CLIENT_DELIVERABLE/MATRIX_CELL/REQUEST transitions; self-registering adapter registry; sendToClient + respondToDeliverable service (5 guarantees, apply opt-in); public respond + admin remind routes; 3 phase-group flags + catalog; 3 pr-check rules. All dark, zero importers, production behavior unchanged. [DONE 2026-06-02 (PR #1012): spine fully merged + dark — migrations 111/112, shared/types/client-deliverable.ts, store, CLIENT_DELIVERABLE transitions, self-registering adapter registry, sendToClient/respondToDeliverable service. Verified by roadmap audit.]

items.title: Phase 1 — per-type cutover (7 PRs: dual-write → backfill → shadow-compare → flag-flip)
items.notes: Migrate each work type onto the spine one PR at a time, gated behind its phase-group flag. Approval-batch family first (fixes the live B1 destructive write). Not started until Phase 0 is merged + green on staging (phase-per-PR). PR-1a (approval_batch family: seo_edit/audit_issue/schema_item/content_plan_*) DARK machinery landed: 5 self-registering adapters (audit_issue carries the real per-check field map + applyable=false for non-meta checks — B1 fix), send-time dual-write mirror gated on unified-deliverables-approval-family (default off → no-op), deterministic sub-type classifier + idempotent backfill script (scripts/backfill-deliverables-approval.ts, not auto-run). Apply stays disabled (D-apply). No reader/UI changes, no flag flip. Still pending: dual-write on response/apply paths, content_plan matrix-cell guard (CP-K4), nudge cron, backfill→shadow-compare→flag-flip. [UPDATE 2026-06-02: dual-write + backfill scripts built + flag-gated for all 5 physical families (PR-1a..1fg, #1013-#1018). REMAINING = shadow-compare (not yet built) -> owner-gated flag-flip -> backfill auto-run -> nudge cron. OWNER-GATED; the B1 destructive-write fix lands at the flip.] || ROADMAP AUDIT 2026-06-11: confirmed complete. Unified deliverable spine is canonical with adapters, backfills, retired rollout flags, unified read/respond services, and integration coverage.

items.title: Phase 2a — client unified inbox DARK behind unified-inbox flag (PriorityStrip + uniform Approve / Request changes / Decline)
items.notes: Strangler-fig client UI: additive flag-gated unified path in InboxTab (flag off → existing new-inbox-ia + legacy layouts byte-for-byte unchanged). New GET /api/public/deliverables/:workspaceId read endpoint assembles physical client_deliverable rows + projected copy/content_request (via adapter projectFromSource), filtered to client-facing statuses, Zod-validated response. NormalizedDecision generalized (add kind + widen DecisionSource to include 'deliverable', isSingleAction kept for back-compat) + normalizeDeliverable() adapter. useUnifiedInbox React Query hook (client- prefixed key, typed api wrapper) fetches only when flag on. When on, InboxTab renders PriorityStrip (mounted — was orphaned) as the single Needs-your-attention list with uniform Approve / Request changes (+note) / Decline calling the real PATCH /respond endpoint (dark). No flag flip; dual-write flags untouched. [DONE 2026-06-02 (PR #1019 + R2-R5 #1025-#1029 + finalize #1031): DARK unified client inbox — read endpoint, useUnifiedInbox flag-gated, PriorityStrip, uniform verbs, inline review, single-item modals. Cutover (flag-flip) tracked separately in phase-1-cutover. Verified by roadmap audit.]

items.title: Phase 2b — admin unified "Client Deliverables" pane DARK behind unified-inbox flag (status axis + stale + Remind)
items.notes: Strangler-fig admin UI (additive — AdminInbox/ClientActionsTab untouched on the flag-off path). New admin read endpoint GET /api/deliverables/:workspaceId (requireWorkspaceAccess, NOT requireAuth) returns EVERY status (physical + projected) via new listAllWorkspaceDeliverables() (the client-facing filter factored out into a shared assembleAllDeliverables), annotated by server/domains/inbox/admin-inbox-read.ts with statusAxis (awaiting_client/changes_requested[+partial]/approved/other), ageDays from sentAt, and a derived stale flag (awaiting_client + ageDays>=STALE_AWAITING_DAYS=7). Zod-validated response (shared/types/admin-deliverable-view.ts). New ClientDeliverablesPane.tsx groups by axis (oldest-first), shows pending-N-days/stale styling + type badge + a Remind button per awaiting_client item wired to the existing POST /api/deliverables/:ws/:id/remind. useWorkspaceDeliverables hook (admin- key, typed adminDeliverables api wrapper, no raw fetch, flag-gated fetch) + useWorkspaceEvents DELIVERABLE_* invalidation. Mounted dark as the first sub-tab in App.tsx's requests TabBar only when unified-inbox is ON (single additive branch: spread an empty tab array + skip the render when off). No flag flip; dual-write flags untouched. DEFERRED to cutover runbook: operator revise/resend/acknowledge on changes_requested (B18/E7 — needs new mutate endpoints), notification-bell categorization (E3/A2), workspace-overview B29 double-count, the read-path slice cutover.

items.title: R2 — respond propagation: unified respondToDeliverable writes back to the SOURCE artifact (DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Fixed the linchpin silent no-op: respondToDeliverable previously wrote ONLY client_deliverable.status, so a unified-inbox client Approve never touched the real source artifact the operator/apply logic reads. Added an optional respondToSource(workspaceId, deliverable, decision, opts) to the adapter interface; every decision-bearing PHYSICAL adapter implements it (approval_batch family seo_edit/audit_issue/schema_item/content_plan_* → legacy approval batch; client_action family redirect/internal_link/aeo_change/content_decay → legacy client_action; schema_plan → schema_site_plans). Each maps the deliverable back to its source id via payload (legacyBatchId / legacyActionId / externalRef=siteId) and reuses the EXISTING source-write logic — extracted two route-inline bodies into shared services: server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-respond.ts (respondToApprovalBatch — whole-batch approve→items approved / changes_requested|declined→items rejected, the team email + APPROVAL_UPDATE broadcast + activity) now called by BOTH the public PATCH /api/public/approvals/:ws/:batch/approve route AND the unified path; server/domains/schema/schema-plan-feedback.ts (respondToSchemaPlanFeedback — approve→client_approved / request_changes→client_changes_requested + activity + SCHEMA_PLAN_SENT broadcast, re-exported by the route-facing `schema-plan-lifecycle.ts` facade) now called by BOTH the public schema-plan /feedback route AND the unified path; client_action reuses the existing respondToPublicClientAction (lazy import to break the adapter↔mutations module cycle). respondToDeliverable now calls adapter.respondToSource after writing the mirror and SUPPRESSES its own deliverable-level team email when the source path reports handled (no double-notify); the DELIVERABLE_UPDATED broadcast is always kept for the unified inbox UI. R2 propagates only the DECISION/status — apply (Webflow publish) stays a separate step (R3, D-apply unchanged). work_order/briefing (no client decision, R5) and projected copy/content_request (R4) get no respondToSource; respond on a projected/unknown id still 404s. Tests: tests/integration/respond-propagation.test.ts seeds BOTH a client_deliverable mirror AND its source artifact per family and asserts (a) the SOURCE status changed, (b) the mirror status changed, (c) team-notify fired exactly once; contract guard extended in tests/contract/inbox-route-service-extraction.test.ts. No flag flip; DARK behind unified-inbox (the unified respond is only reachable when the inbox is on).

items.title: R3a — deliverable detail + per-item review: surface the substance in the unified inbox + flag/subset-approve (DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Fixed the owner complaint that the unified inbox sends the task, not the content. The card's 'View N →' now opens a new full-screen DeliverableDetailModal (src/components/client/DeliverableDetailModal.tsx) rendering the SUBSTANCE from the deliverable's carried NormalizedDecision.items + .payload (no second fetch). REUSED the proven legacy renderers by EXTRACTING them into src/components/client/decision-renderers.tsx (ApprovalItemRow, AeoRenderer, InternalLinkRenderer, RedirectRenderer) plus a new presentational ItemDiffRow that both the legacy DecisionDetailModal and the new modal drive (DRY); legacy modal behavior unchanged. Approval family (seo_edit/audit_issue/schema_item/content_plan_*) → typed items[] rows with PER-ITEM Flag/Unflag + 'Looks good — implement N of M →' subset CTA; client_action family (redirect/internal_link/aeo_change) → payload.items fed read-only to the matching renderer wrapper with whole-action Approve/Request changes/Decline (no per-item flag — matches legacy). One respond path: modal approve forwards flagged ClientDeliverableItem.id`s through the EXISTING useRespondToDeliverable → PATCH /respond (new optional flaggedItemIds: z.array(z.string()).max(500).optional()), extending R2. Server: respondToApprovalBatch gained an optional per-item mode (itemDecisions: {legacyItemId,status,note}[]) — when present, drives updateItem per legacy item (unflagged→approved, flagged→rejected) inside the existing db.transaction(); absent → whole-batch approve-all-pending (back-compat). respondToApprovalBatchSource builds itemDecisions from deliverable.items (item→legacy approval_item.id via itemPayload.legacyItemId, skip+warn on no mapping) only on approved with non-empty flags; the mirror status stays approved (implement N of M); changes_requested/declined ignore flags. Per-item flag notes thread onto each held item's clientNote. content_decay stays inline single-action; projected types keep their read-only deep-link. Confirmed getDeliverable() DOES load items so responded.items carries itemPayload.legacyItemId. NOT the Apply-to-Website PR (that is R3b — Webflow apply unchanged/disabled, D-apply). Tests: tests/integration/respond-per-item-propagation.test.ts (port-free service-level; subset approve, multi-flag, back-compat approve-all, empty-array back-compat, reject-ignores-flags, client_action-ignores-flags) + tests/unit/DeliverableDetailModal.test.tsx (deliverable→renderer mapping per family + per-item flag CTA + onApprove carries flagged id). No flag flip; DARK behind unified-inbox.

items.title: R3b — Apply to Website: explicit post-approve publish step in the unified inbox, reusing the EXISTING Webflow apply route (DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Owner requirement: a SEPARATE, explicit 'Apply to Website' step after approve that goes through the proven Webflow apply route (ZERO new apply logic). The client calls the SAME POST /api/public/approvals/:ws/:batch/apply route the legacy ApprovalBatchCard uses (it already does updatePageSeo/updateCollectionItem/publishCollectionItems, the server-side applyability gate, markBatchApplied, activity, outcome tracking, broadcasts). Added ONLY: (1) shared/applyability.ts — the SINGLE source of truth applyability predicate (field/targetRef/collectionId), intentionally mirroring the LEGACY ROUTE GATE, NOT the per-item applyable column (which is hardcoded false for the whole approval family under D-apply); the legacy src/components/client/approvalApplyability.ts now delegates to it (behavior-preserving), and the server route gate (server/routes/approvals.ts) + its local isCmsSeoApprovalField were replaced with isClientApplyableFields (the two in-loop cms- guards kept as defense-in-depth). (2) Server mirror-flip markDeliverableApplied (server/domains/inbox/send-to-client.ts) — body of applyApprovedDeliverable MINUS the external apply() call (Webflow write already done by the legacy route), idempotent on already-applied; called from the /apply route AFTER markBatchApplied, gated on APPROVAL_FAMILY_FLAG (NOT unified-inbox), wrapped in try/catch that logs+swallows (a mirror miss must not fail the apply response). isFeatureEnabled is single-arg/global. The disabled applyDisabledStub stays wired (appliesOnApprove omitted) — D-apply holds; the mirror-flip does NOT call adapter.applyDeliverable. (3) Client: publicDeliverables.applyApproval wrapper + useApplyDeliverable hook (invalidates the unified inbox key); a new 'Ready to publish' section in UnifiedInbox.tsx (approved + client-applyable deliverables, since approved is NOT in ACTIONABLE_STATUSES but IS client-facing) rendering an 'Apply to Website' primary button (ConfirmDialog 'Apply to live site?') + 'View N changes →'; DeliverableDetailModal gained canApply/applying/onApply props rendering a single full-width Apply button instead of the approve/decline row. Post-apply the deliverable flips to applied (filtered OUT of CLIENT_FACING_STATUSES) so the item LEAVES the inbox + the modal auto-unmounts (intended UX; success toast confirms). Held/subset-rejected items are never applied (the route's status==='approved' filter excludes them). schema_item/schema_plan/content_plan_*/client_action get NO button (the shared predicate excludes them; the route gate rejects them). Tests: tests/integration/r3b-apply-to-website.test.ts (5: applyable seo_edit flips mirror, subset-held not applied + mirror still flips, schema_item 400 no flip, flag-off byte-identical no flip, markDeliverableApplied idempotency) + tests/unit/applyability-shared.test.ts (incl. the documented divergence: applyable:false items still canApply=true) + DeliverableDetailModal Apply-footer cases. No flag flip; DARK behind unified-inbox + APPROVAL_FAMILY_FLAG.

items.title: R4 — in-shell projected review: mount the bespoke copy/content review surfaces inside the unified inbox so the client reviews + responds without leaving (DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Owner requirement: the two PROJECTED deliverable types (copy_section / content_request) had no physical row; R3a rendered them as a read-only 'Review →' deep-link that navigated the client OUT to ?tab=reviews. R4 mounts the PROVEN bespoke review surfaces IN-SHELL so the client reviews + responds 'once and for all' without leaving. Added ONLY: (1) src/components/client/inbox/ProjectedReviewModal.tsx — a thin full-screen modal wrapper (chrome + Escape-to-close guard copied verbatim from DeliverableDetailModal, incl. the isContentEditable/input/textarea/select guard so the RichTextEditor + useAutoSave inside PostReviewCard never lose typing on a stray Escape; WIDE max-w-5xl panel since ContentTab renders a PageHeader + 4-col stat grid + pipeline list). Branches on TYPE (never deliverable status): copy_section → <ClientCopyReview initialExpandedEntryId={externalRef}/>; content_request → <ContentTab {...passThrough} initialExpandedRequestId={externalRef}/> (ContentTab self-selects brief actions vs PostReviewCard from the LOCAL ClientContentRequest.status — both client_review brief + post_review post map to canonical awaiting_client, the raw status lives only in payload.contentRequestStatus). (2) Additive OPTIONAL props on the shared bespoke components (legacy-safe, default = current behavior): ClientCopyReview.initialExpandedEntryId (threaded outer wrapper → inner → seeds expandedEntryId useState); ContentTab.initialExpandedRequestId → useContentRequests seeds expandedContentReq. PostReviewCard unchanged (reached transitively); DecisionCard.onReview contract unchanged (only the callback body swapped in UnifiedInbox). (3) UnifiedInbox: reviewProjected state; projected onReview now opens the modal (reading raw d.type + d.externalRef — normalizeDeliverable drops both) instead of goToReviews (deleted, with its dead navigate/betaMode locals + useNavigate/useBetaMode/clientPath imports — tsc noUnusedLocals confirms zero unused); modal mounted near DeliverableDetailModal; UnifiedInboxProps extended with the ContentTab pass-through props (Omit<ContentTabProps,'workspaceId'|'setToast'|'initialExpandedRequestId'>), threaded from ClientDashboard → InboxTab (NOT hasCopyEntries — it's a ClientCopyReview gate, not a ContentTab prop). (4) MANDATORY unified-list refresh: the bespoke respond routes broadcast COPY_SECTION_UPDATED / CONTENT_REQUEST_UPDATE / POST_UPDATED, which UnifiedInbox did NOT listen on — added 3 additive handlers (flag-ON-only) each invalidating queryKeys.client.unifiedInbox so after an in-shell respond the card leaves the list + the modal can be dismissed; all 3 events already broadcast + handled elsewhere so broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts stays green. Respond NEVER hits the unified /respond (projected ids 404 there — getDeliverable returns null → SendToClientError 404). BetaProvider confirmed already above the modal (ClientDashboard wraps the dashboard at :637). Tests: updated tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx (the 2 projected tests now assert 'Review →' OPENS the in-shell modal + does NOT navigate, with real externalRefs); new tests/component/ProjectedReviewModal.test.tsx (copy→ClientCopyReview auto-expanded, content→ContentTab auto-expanded + pass-through, Escape guarded in editable / closes elsewhere, backdrop close); new tests/integration/projected-review-respond-routes.test.ts (port 13877: content-request approve emits CONTENT_REQUEST_UPDATE, copy approve emits COPY_SECTION_UPDATED, + NEGATIVE projected content_request:/copy: ids 404 on unified /respond). No flag flip; DARK behind unified-inbox (flag-off + legacy ?tab=reviews surfaces byte-identical).

items.title: R5 — work_order TRACK lane: read-only order-progress lane in the unified inbox (NO approve/decline verbs, DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Owner decision: work orders must LIVE in the unified inbox but as a read/TRACK lane — STATUS the client FOLLOWS (work the agency does FOR them), NOT a decision they make → ZERO approve/decline/apply/review verbs. (briefing stays OUT — it's surfaced on the dashboard homepage, not the inbox.) CRITICAL pre-fix: work_order rows were NOT client-facing today — work_order statuses are ordered|in_progress|completed|cancelled, NONE of which were in CLIENT_FACING_STATUSES, so they were filtered OUT of the client list. R5 needed BOTH a server read change AND a client render path. SERVER (server/domains/inbox/unified-inbox-read.ts): added CLIENT_FACING_ORDER_STATUSES={ordered,in_progress,completed} (cancelled excluded) + a KIND-AWARE predicate isClientFacingDeliverable(d) that admits the order-lifecycle statuses ONLY for kind:'order' and uses isClientFacingDeliverableStatus for every other kind; listClientFacingDeliverables now filters on it. isClientFacingDeliverableStatus stays exported+unchanged (other callers); listAllWorkspaceDeliverables (admin pane) untouched (already returns all statuses). Gated on KIND, not type, so no review/decision/batch row can leak ordered/in_progress/completed into the client list. The work_order adapter + dual-write needed NO change (they already produce correct rows — they were just filtered out). CLIENT (src/components/client/inbox/UnifiedInbox.tsx): added module-level ORDER_STATUS_CHIP (Four Laws — ordered=amber, in_progress=blue+Loader2 spin, completed=emerald; NEVER teal for the chip) + OrderTrackStepper over the canonical steps [ordered,in_progress,completed] (completed-step dots/connectors use teal to mark completed PROGRESS, a data affordance, consistent with the legacy OrderStatus stepper) + a workOrders useMemo (deliverables.filter kind==='order'). New dedicated read-only <section> 'Work in progress' (mirrors the R3b 'Ready to publish' container: surface-2 + radius-signature-lg w/ the pr-check-disable-next-line brand-radius hatch). Each card: status chip, title, summary, OrderTrackStepper (SKIPPED for completed), age (ageLabel), and a COUNT-ONLY page summary ('N pages') — raw payload.pageIds are raw Webflow ids and are NEVER surfaced (CLAUDE.md 'never surface raw IDs'). The card is PLAIN markup: NO normalizeDeliverable, NO DecisionCard, NO onApprove/onFlagWithNote/onDecline/onReview/onApply wired (structural verb-safety — there is no verb to wire). Orders never enter `actionable` (ACTIONABLE_STATUSES has no order status) so they never reach the PriorityStrip; PriorityStrip's sectionForKind case 'order' left as-is (unreachable). Orders-only empty-state FIX: the 'Nothing needs your attention' guard is now actionable.length===0 && workOrders.length===0 so it does NOT show when there are orders to track. NOTE (intentional, out-of-scope): the legacy src/components/client/OrderStatus.tsx + its route /api/public/work-orders/:ws stay LIVE — R5 introduces a SECOND client-facing surface for work-order data (the unified track lane) and intentionally does NOT retire the legacy component/route; reconciliation is a future follow-up. No new flag (DARK behind unified-inbox fetch flag + unified-deliverables-rest mirror flag, both default false). Tests: tests/integration/work-order-track-lane-read.test.ts (port 13878: kind:'order' in ordered/in_progress/completed INCLUDED, cancelled EXCLUDED, CROSS-LEAK guard seo_edit/batch in in_progress/ordered EXCLUDED, review-status rows still included, client-portal auth still 401s) + extended tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx (track section renders title/summary/chip/stepper, ZERO verbs + mutations never called, order absent from PriorityStrip, completed renders chip but no stepper, orders-only empty-state suppressed, flag OFF no section).

items.title: Work-order conversation (client↔team comments) + explicit `closed` state + admin conversation/close panel (PRODUCTION data model/routes/admin; client thread DARK behind unified-inbox)
items.notes: Owner decisions: focused admin conversation/close panel (NOT a full dashboard tab); email both ways; dedicated comments table; operator-only close; completed→closed one-way (NO reopen); comments OUT-OF-BAND from the deliverable payload. DARK SPLIT — PRODUCTION (not flag-gated): the data model, state machine, migration, store, routes, notifications, the mirror exhaustive-switch arm, and the ADMIN conversation/close panel. DARK (behind unified-inbox): only the CLIENT-facing conversation thread + comment input inside the 'Work in progress' track-lane card. STATE MACHINE (server/state-machines.ts): WORK_ORDER_TRANSITIONS completed→['closed'] (was []), closed:[] terminal; added 'closed' to WorkOrderStatus. No completed→in_progress reopen. SHARED (shared/types/payments.ts): 'closed' added to WorkOrder.status; optional closedAt?; new WorkOrderComment{id,workOrderId,author:'client'|'team',content,createdAt,readAt?} + WorkOrderCommentAuthor. MIGRATION 113-work-order-comments.sql: CREATE TABLE work_order_comments (FK→work_orders + workspaces, both ON DELETE CASCADE) + 2 indexes + ALTER TABLE work_orders ADD COLUMN closed_at (the runner detects ADD COLUMN → per-statement exec w/ duplicate-column tolerance, so mixed CREATE+ALTER is safe; append-only). STORE: closed_at lockstep in server/work-orders.ts (OrderRow/insert/update/rowToOrder/createWorkOrder writes null/updateWorkOrder accepts closedAt). New server/work-order-comments.ts (sibling module): listWorkOrderComments + addWorkOrderComment (null if parent missing; the read+insert+parent-updated_at bump wrapped in db.transaction(...).immediate() — BEGIN IMMEDIATE avoids the SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT read-then-write race); own stmt-cache + rowToComment (three-state read_at). Comments are NOT a field on WorkOrder/OrderRow (served out-of-band). ROUTES: admin PATCH (server/routes/work-orders.ts) added 'closed' to the Zod enum + a closed branch (set closedAt + order_closed activity; does NOT re-enter the completed page-state/email block); admin comment POST (author 'team') + GET; client public comment POST (server/routes/public-content.ts, behind the router-level requireClientPortalAuth prefix gate, author HARDCODED 'client', 409 when closed) + public GET. New server/schemas/work-orders.ts workOrderCommentSchema {content: string.min(1).max(2000)}.strict(). EVENTS: WS_EVENTS.WORK_ORDER_COMMENT='work-order:comment' (server + frontend mirror). ACTIVITY: 'work_order_commented' + 'order_closed' added to ActivityType + CLIENT_VISIBLE_TYPES. EMAIL: notifyTeamWorkOrderComment (client→team, internal) + notifyClientWorkOrderComment (team→client, action) + 2 templates + email-throttle category map entries; best-effort, never blocks the write. MIRROR (server/domains/inbox/deliverable-adapters/work-order.ts): 'closed' case → 'cancelled' (leaves the client lane, ∉ CLIENT_FACING_ORDER_STATUSES; raw status preserved in payload.workOrderStatus) — satisfies the exhaustive never-guard; completed stays 'completed' (stays in lane). ADMIN PANEL: src/components/admin/WorkOrderPanel.tsx — focused full-screen modal (mirrors DeliverableDetailModal chrome) opened from WorkspaceHome's 'purchased fixes awaiting fulfillment' attention item (the item now carries onClick→setWorkOrderPanelOpen instead of navigating to workspace-settings). Lists orders → select → order detail + client↔team conversation + reply box (author 'team') + Start work / Mark complete / Close out (ConfirmDialog, destructive). No purple (not AI). New src/api/work-orders.ts (workOrders + publicWorkOrders typed wrappers) + src/hooks/admin/useWorkOrders.ts (list/comments/status PATCH/comment mutation). queryKeys.admin.workOrders + workOrderComments. CLIENT LANE (DARK): the 'Work in progress' card's map body extracted into a module-level WorkOrderTrackCard sub-component (so a hook can run per card) that renders the existing verb-free card PLUS the client↔team conversation thread (public GET via useClientWorkOrderComments) + a comment input (public POST via usePostClientWorkOrderComment, author 'client'). Order id extracted from sourceRef ('work_order:<id>'). Input gated on workOrderStatus !== 'closed' (closed leaves the lane anyway). Lane stays verb-free — NO approve/decline/apply; the conversation input is the ONLY interactive element. queryKeys.client.workOrderComments. WS HANDLERS: WORK_ORDER_COMMENT handled on BOTH the admin side (useWsInvalidation → admin workOrderComments+workOrders) and the client side (UnifiedInbox wsHandlers → client workOrderComments + unified inbox) so broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts stays green. TESTS: state-machine (completed→closed legal, closed terminal, illegal in_progress→closed/closed→* rejected; existing assertions updated); store unit (work-order-comments.test.ts: insert/null-missing-parent/ordered/scope/closedAt); routes integration (work-order-conversation-close.test.ts port 13409: admin PATCH→closed sets closedAt+activity, admin comment POST author team + GET, client public comment forces 'client'+409-when-closed+401-unauth-on-pw-workspace+.strict()-rejects-author, illegal close 400); mirror arm in work-order-adapter.test.ts (closed→cancelled); client lane component (InboxTabUnified.test.tsx: conversation thread+input render for non-closed w/ sourceRef, post calls mutation, input hidden when closed, still no decision verbs). NOTE: the deferred unified-inbox-deferred-ia-polish item (a) WORK_ORDER realtime gap is partially addressed — the WORK_ORDER_COMMENT handler invalidates the unified inbox; the WORK_ORDER_UPDATE→unified-inbox invalidation + completed-order lane decay remain in that item.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Completed items from Intelligence Quality Consolidation — Recommendations & AI Content

items.title: AI/recommendation context pre-plan audit + guardrails
items.source: Platform insights + AI content audit discussion 2026-05-18
